title: six6 Deprecated Decision V2
description: One JSONL record produced by human review when a memory candidate is rejected or retired from the approval path. It keeps the audit boundary minimal and links to the fuller human-readable deprecated_decisions.md log when present.

candidate_id.description: Candidate-layer stable identifier used to audit which candidate was deprecated.

topic.description: Candidate topic retained so deprecation logs remain readable without reopening the candidate batch.

deprecation_reason.description: Concise reason the candidate should not enter approved memory ingestion.

source_file.description: Physical source file retained for audit lookup.

decided_by.description: Decision origin for audit and weekly sampling.

$comment: Deprecated Decision V2 is the machine-readable rejection record. deprecated_decisions.md may provide narrative review notes, but this JSONL contract is the stable audit key/value boundary.
